summary: CONSTITUTIONAL CHECKS: ALL 4 PASS — Cantonal (dispatch_event_log is SUPPORTING, not CANONICAL/ERROR; lcs_canonical+lcs_errors cantonal pair untouched), Registry-First (CTB pre-registration at Section 1 line 26 before CREATE TABLE at Section 3 line 49), Sovereign ID (no minting — fn_record_dispatch_result operates on existing MIDs; event_id gen_random_uuid is operational PK not sovereign identity), No sideways calls (all reads/writes within lcs schema: mid_ledger, dispatch_event_log, lcs_errors). ENVELOPE: PASS — all 4 fields match (change_type=feature, architectural_flag=false, doctrine_version=2.8.0). SCOPE: PASS — 2 modified paths within migrations/ and tests/. No protected assets touched. EXECUTION: PASS — all 6 acceptance criteria met. (1) fn_record_dispatch_result(uuid, text, text, text, jsonb) created as VOID-returning entry point. Steps: FOR UPDATE lock, validate SENT, validate result_state in {DELIVERED,FAILED,BOUNCED}, update mid_ledger, insert event log. (2) Three validation layers: SENT state check (STATE_VIOLATION error + return), result_state check (VALIDATION error + return), forward-only FSM via existing fn_dispatch_state_forward_only trigger from Phase 3A (reused, not duplicated). (3) mid_ledger UPDATE sets dispatch_state + provider + external_message_id + dispatch_result + finalized_at + CASE-based delivered_at/failed_at/bounced_at. Event log INSERT records mid + provider + external_message_id + result_state + metadata. (4) dispatch_event_log created with event_id UUID PK, mid FK, provider/external_message_id/result_state/metadata/recorded_at. Append-only: no-UPDATE trigger + no-DELETE trigger. CTB registered as SUPPORTING. CHECK constraint on result_state. (5) lcs_transport role created (NOLOGIN, idempotent). EXECUTE granted on function. REVOKE from PUBLIC. REVOKE ALL on 5 tables (mid_ledger, dispatch_event_log, lcs_errors, lcs_canonical, worker_run_log) — exceeds WP's 2-table requirement for comprehensive isolation. (6) 15 tests across 3 categories (CS claims 14, actual 15 — minor undercount non-blocking): dispatch_event_log structure (4: exists, CTB SUPPORTING, update blocked, delete blocked), fn_record_dispatch_result behavior (7: DELIVERED/FAILED/BOUNCED updates with timestamp verification, event log insert, rejects non-SENT, rejects invalid result_state, MID not found error), permissions (4: role exists, EXECUTE on function, no INSERT on mid_ledger, no INSERT on dispatch_event_log). OBSERVATIONS: (1) No RAISE-after-DML confirmed — error paths INSERT+RETURN before UPDATE; success path UPDATE fires BEFORE trigger (RAISE during DML not after); if blocked, entire function rolls back with no orphaned DML. (2) dispatch_result and finalized_at columns pre-exist from earlier migrations — not in Phase 3C ALTER TABLE additions but verified working by tests. (3) Defense-in-depth: CHECK constraint on dispatch_event_log provides DB-level result_state validation even if function IF check bypassed. (4) SECURITY INVOKER gap consistent with 2F/3A/3B — works as owner, needs SECURITY DEFINER for full least-privilege.
